other_observers: Brenda Wright and many other birders came to the the sparrow during the 8 days
specific_location: Clifton Wildlife Park at the Colorado River
description: Sparrow would only feed from the ground feeder where seeds were spread on the grown. Sparrow was the same size as the White-crowned Sparrows it was with. Song Sparrows and Juncos were also present. I first noticed the bill was dark and not yellow. The the face lacked the strong eye-line due to a grayer overall face color then the White-crowns. When the bird faced me I could clearly see a wide space between the brown head stripes and a gold/yellow patch at the leading edge. The rest of the field marks pretty much matched the first winter White-crowned Sparrows. The Golden-crowned was always easy to pick out among the ground feeders even though its differences were not bold. We never hear the bird call.
